businessSummary.oneLiner: The world's largest EV maker and a 'Physical AI Platform' spanning autonomous driving, robotics, energy, and custom chips
businessSummary.description: Tesla has expanded well beyond electric vehicles into FSD autonomous driving, Cybercab robotaxis, the Optimus humanoid robot, and Megapack energy storage. The company posted $94.8 billion in FY2025 revenue with GAAP EPS of $1.08, and Q4 2025 gross margin hit 20.1%, a two-year high. Key catalysts are set to materialize from April 2026 with Cybercab production and Optimus Gen 3 unveiling.
howTheyMakeMoney: Energy storage and generation (Megapack, Powerwall, Solar)
Services and charging (Supercharger network, Insurance, OTA upgrades)

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Extreme premium vs. S&P 500 avg (~23x) and auto sector avg (~16x). Slightly improved from 235x on the price drop, but still deeply elevated

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on FY2026 EPS consensus of $2.53. UBS cut Q1 delivery estimates citing demand softening

keyMetrics.name: Gross Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Q4 2025 hit a two-year high of 20.1%. Margin recovery signal despite ongoing pricing pressure

keyMetrics.name: Revenue (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: FY2025 annual revenue. Slight YoY decline but energy segment driving growth

keyMetrics.name: FCF (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Healthy cash generation. $44B total cash provides ample runway for capex

keyMetrics.name: Net Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Compressed due to price competition and elevated R&D spending

growthDrivers.title: Samsung AI6 Chip: $16.5B Foundry Deal Confirmed
growthDrivers.description: Samsung confirmed at its AGM that Tesla's AI6 (HW6) chip will be mass-produced at its Taylor, TX fab using 2nm (SF2P) process through 2033. However, per Electrek (3/12), AI6 MPW (prototype) production is delayed roughly 6 months due to Samsung yield issues.

growthDrivers.title: Terafab Semiconductor Unit Officially Launched: $20B+ AI Chip Fab
growthDrivers.description: Tesla officially announced its Terafab semiconductor division targeting hundreds of billions of custom chips annually. The strategy is to produce AI training, FSD inference, and Optimus robot chips in-house to reduce external dependency and improve margins.

growthDrivers.title: LG Energy Solution $4.3B LFP Battery Plant Deal Confirmed
growthDrivers.description: The US government confirmed the Michigan Lansing LFP cell manufacturing facility contract. US-made cells for Megapack 3 with production starting 2027. Unit capacity reaches 5 MWh (28% increase from 3.9 MWh).

growthDrivers.title: Cybercab Production Starting April, Macrohard AI Venture
growthDrivers.description: Cybercab production line designed for hundreds of units per week. Macrohard (Tesla-xAI joint venture) targets enterprise AI platforms, diversifying revenue beyond automotive.

growthDrivers.title: China Jan-Feb Sales +35% YoY, Global Recovery Signal
growthDrivers.description: Tesla EVs made in China sold 127,728 units in Jan-Feb, up 35%+ YoY. BYD numbers actually declined, signaling a reversal in China market dynamics.

competitiveAdvantage.summary: US EV market leader with 50%+ share. 8.2 billion FSD miles, Supercharger NACS standard, and vertical integration form the core moat. Samsung AI6 foundry deal accelerates in-house chip ecosystem.

moats.description: 8.2 billion FSD miles of driving data, far exceeding any competitor's training dataset.
Proprietary batteries (4680), custom chips (Terafab + Samsung AI6), software (FSD), and charging infrastructure (Supercharger).
NACS is the North American EV charging standard. GM, Ford, and Rivian all rely on Tesla Superchargers.

competitors.name: BYD
competitors.detail: Global EV sales leader (2.26M in 2025 vs Tesla's 1.64M). 11 new models, 640-mile sedan, and 5-minute charging tech unveiled

competitors.name: Rivian (RIVN)
competitors.detail: Electric pickup/SUV maker. Amazon logistics partner

recentNews.headline: NHTSA Escalates FSD Probe to Engineering Analysis: 3.2M Vehicles, Recall Possible
recentNews.significance: NHTSA upgraded its investigation into FSD safety under reduced-visibility conditions to a full engineering analysis. After 9 crashes (1 fatal), a mandatory recall is now on the table, adding significant uncertainty to Tesla's autonomous driving strategy.

recentNews.headline: Terafab Semiconductor Division Officially Announced: Targeting Hundreds of Billions of Custom Chips
recentNews.significance: Tesla formalized its in-house chip fabrication business starting with AI5. A long-term play to reduce external dependency and improve margins, but requires $20B+ in capital investment.

recentNews.headline: Fed Holds Rates at 3.5-3.75%, Projects Only 1 Cut in 2026: Markets Sell Off
recentNews.significance: The Fed held rates steady and reduced 2026 cut projections to just once. S&P 500 and Nasdaq fell 1.4%, hitting growth stocks hard. Particularly unfavorable for TSLA's stretched valuation.

recentNews.headline: CUSMA Trade Deal Review Begins: Mexico Sourcing Costs May Rise
recentNews.significance: CUSMA renegotiations have started with USTR focusing on stricter rules of origin to curb Asian transshipment via Mexico. Tighter rules could raise Tesla's Mexico-sourced component costs.

recentNews.headline: UBS Cuts Q1 Delivery Estimates: Demand Softening and Robotaxi Competition Concerns
recentNews.significance: UBS lowered Q1 delivery forecasts citing core market demand softening and intensifying robotaxi competition. GLJ Research also reaffirmed its Sell rating post-FSD probe.

analystOpinions.notableComment: MarketBeat consensus of $406.84 across 41 analysts (Hold). BNP Paribas cut to $280 (Underperform), BofA initiated at $460 (Buy), Wedbush maintains $600 (Outperform). Robotaxi execution is the key variable.

risks.title: NHTSA FSD Engineering Analysis: Mandatory Recall Risk
risks.description: Investigation covering 3.2M vehicles escalated to engineering analysis. 9 crashes (1 fatal) in reduced-visibility conditions. A mandatory hardware recall would be a direct blow to the robotaxi strategy.

risks.title: Fed Rate Hold + 2026 Cuts Reduced to 1
risks.description: Fed held at 3.5-3.75% with only 1 cut projected this year. Seven FOMC members expect no cuts at all. The worst possible rate environment for a stock trading at 228x P/E.

risks.title: BYD Competition Accelerating, Global EV Share Reversal
risks.description: BYD sold 2.26M vs Tesla's 1.64M in 2025. 11 new models, a 640-mile sedan, and 5-minute charging tech are narrowing the technology gap.

risks.title: UBS Q1 Delivery Cut + GLJ Sell Reaffirmation
risks.description: UBS lowered Q1 estimates on demand softening and robotaxi competition. GLJ reaffirmed Sell after the FSD probe. Selling pressure mounting.

risks.title: Valuation Strain at 228x P/E
risks.description: 10x premium over the S&P 500 average of 23x. Delayed rate cuts heighten multiple compression risk.

risks.title: CUSMA Review: Mexico Supply Chain Risk
risks.description: CUSMA trade deal review could tighten rules of origin, raising costs for Tesla's Mexico-sourced components.

risks.title: Terafab Capital Intensity Risk
risks.description: Semiconductor fabs require massive capital investment ($20B+). Long-term margin improvement potential but near-term cash outflow pressure.

buyReasons.title: Terafab: The Dawn of In-House Semiconductor Production
buyReasons.rationale: No other automaker can produce its own chips. If successful, completes the vertical integration of AI, FSD, and Optimus.

buyReasons.title: Samsung AI6 $16.5B + LG $4.3B: Chip and Battery Supply Chain Secured
buyReasons.rationale: Locking in both semiconductor and battery supply chains domestically strengthens long-term competitiveness.

buyReasons.title: China Jan-Feb +35%, Energy Business at Record Profitability
buyReasons.rationale: China market rebound and energy storage growth offset EV core business softening.

buyReasons.title: Cybercab Production Starting in April
buyReasons.rationale: Robotaxi mass production is imminent. If successful, creates an entirely new revenue stream.

overallOpinion: NHTSA escalated its FSD probe to engineering analysis, putting 3.2 million vehicles at risk of mandatory recall. With autonomous driving at the core of Tesla's long-term thesis, regulatory risk has intensified significantly.
The Fed held rates and cut 2026 projections to just one reduction, sending the S&P down 1.4%. For a stock at 228x P/E, this is the worst macro backdrop. UBS also lowered Q1 delivery estimates.

At $380.45, Tesla sits below its 200-day SMA ($394) with RSI at 33 in oversold territory. Down 22% from its 52-week high. Terafab and April Cybercab production are real catalysts, but macro and regulatory headwinds are stronger right now.

investmentVerdict.summary: The NHTSA FSD probe escalation and Fed rate cut reduction hit simultaneously, intensifying downward pressure. Terafab and Cybercab April production are legitimate catalysts, but at 228x P/E below the 200-day SMA with RSI at 33, chasing the stock before regulatory uncertainty clears is risky. Safer to wait for FSD probe outcomes and Q1 earnings.

threads.hook: Tesla slides on NHTSA escalation and Fed rate hold
threads.text:
$TSLA $380.45 (-3.14%)

NHTSA escalated its FSD investigation to a full engineering analysis. 3.2 million vehicles are under review after 9 crashes (1 fatal) in reduced-visibility conditions. A mandatory recall is now a real possibility, casting a shadow over Tesla's entire autonomous driving roadmap.

The Fed held rates at 3.5-3.75% and projected just one cut this year. The S&P fell 1.4%, and for a stock trading at 228x P/E, this is the worst macro environment imaginable. UBS lowered Q1 delivery estimates as well.

On the bright side, Tesla officially launched Terafab, its semiconductor division. No other automaker produces its own chips. Cybercab production starts next month.

Below the 200-day SMA ($394), RSI at 33 in oversold territory. Analyst target $406.84 (+6.9%). Real catalysts exist, but regulatory and macro headwinds are stronger right now. Hold.

$TSLA $380.45 (-3.14%)

NHTSA escalated its FSD investigation to engineering analysis. 3.2M vehicles under review after 9 crashes (1 fatal). Mandatory recall is possible.

Fed held at 3.5-3.75%, projecting just 1 cut this year. S&P fell 1.4%. The worst environment for a 228x P/E stock.

Terafab semiconductor launch and Cybercab April production are bullish, but UBS cut Q1, GLJ reaffirmed Sell. Below 200-day SMA, RSI 33 oversold. Target $406.84 (+6.9%). Hold.

witty.hook: Tesla builds a chip fab. NHTSA questions the software on those chips.
witty.text:
$TSLA $380.45 (-3.14%)

Terafab goes official on the same day NHTSA escalates the FSD probe. You can't write better dramatic irony.

3.2M vehicles under engineering analysis. Fed says 1 cut this year. UBS lowers Q1 deliveries. CUSMA review threatens Mexico sourcing. The bad news is lining up like it booked reservations.

But Terafab is the real deal. Only automaker making its own chips. RSI 33 oversold, down 22% from highs. Is this a sale or a trap? NHTSA will let us know.

Target $406.84 (+6.9%). Hold. Patience is a virtue, especially at 228x P/E.

empathy.hook: An honest word for Tesla shareholders
empathy.text:
$TSLA $380.45 (-3.14%)

From $490 to here. Down 22%. Between the NHTSA probe escalation and the Fed cutting rate expectations, today was rough.

The FSD investigation moving to engineering analysis is concerning. 3.2M vehicles, possible mandatory recall. FSD is Tesla's future, and regulators are pushing back hard. UBS cut Q1 estimates. GLJ reaffirmed Sell.

But Tesla isn't falling apart. Terafab puts Tesla in a league no automaker can follow. Cybercab starts next month. China sales up 35%.

RSI 33 oversold. These levels have triggered rebounds before. But now isn't the time to buy the dip aggressively. Let the NHTSA results and Q1 earnings provide clarity.

Target $406.84 (+6.9%). Hold.
